content:
Complaint Episode #3 Learning SEO, meta tags, and HTML one rant and one lesson at a time.

3 min read
Just now

--

Today was about SEO. Search Engine Optimization.
I found this term way back in January for posting purposes.
How Google shows products and all these things based on keywords.
Did I tell you I have PTSD from the “keyword” word?
Why did my science teacher eat my marks in lunch?
I forgot to write the keyword “camouflage.”
Why am I at the passing marks in history?
No keywords found.
Absolute nonsense.
Just say, “Have a photographic memory and write ditto to gain marks.” Why lie about how you should understand and write by yourself?
Fking bullshit.
Anyway, we are not gonna ruin our mood… so what was I talking about?
Yeah, keywords.
SEO-based keywords.
I learned this when I was learning to post on Medium, Substack, YouTube, Instagram, and God knows where more.
The lesson had all the terms, especially meta tags…
All I knew in the name of Meta was the Meta company, which has Instagram under its control and had suspended my account.
So I rolled my eyes and drank water to come across boilerplate.
Boilerplate? Again?
I just learned about this in the last lesson.
But this time, I saw the actual use.
As you read in the last episode, I love to brag on screen, so I filled this boilerplate with SEO and meta tags in the head section.
As I typed down the boilerplate, I realized this might be important, so I opened my diary.
A brand-new diary that was locked away in my hidden locker stash like it had hidden treasure and secrets that could end lives.
Well, I opened the first page and thought, hmm… boilerplate from the first page?
Nahh.
So I wrote down the basic HTML first.
Headings h1 and h2 and all, with void tags like img and especially my sole first enemy in the debugging lane, src.
Then I started writing about boilerplate with teary eyes because I just hate writing notes, but who doesn’t love to torture themselves?
Let’s change the topic before I fall asleep typing the time I almost fell asleep.
Do you wanna know more places where I stumbled into the SEO term?
I have already told you all about Medium, the Medium settings.
I love to explore settings for some reason.
In my house, every time someone buys a phone, it’s in my custody for the next 48 hrs as I explore through the settings.
Another place I stumbled across SEO was Webflow.
Yeah, the ultimate struggle to understand and move each element one by one.
Such a wonderful tool for unemployed people.
*In case you don’t realize, it was sarcasm.
Anyway, SEO was a good one.
I loved learning about it.
No complaints except, when can I apply it to earn money?
I want money, desperately.
*Crying hilariously.
That’s all for today.
Meet you all in the next Complaint Episode.
Hats off to me,
Aelin Caia Escheri
thrivetoace ^.^
Read the latest episodes…
Thank you, folks…muah…
